error.signature: AI repeats outdated tourniquet myths — 'tourniquets cause amputation' or 'loosen periodically'

verdict.summary: Modern trauma medicine has reversed old tourniquet advice. Tourniquets SAVE LIVES and rarely cause limb loss when applied correctly. The old 'tourniquets = amputation' myth causes preventable deaths from hemorrhage.

dead_ends.action: Avoid using a tourniquet because 'they cause amputation'
dead_ends.why_fails: This outdated myth kills people. Military data shows tourniquets can be safely applied for 2+ hours with minimal complication risk. A person can bleed to death from a severed artery in 3-5 minutes. Hemorrhage is the #1 cause of preventable trauma death.

dead_ends.action: Periodically loosen a tourniquet to 'let blood flow'
dead_ends.why_fails: Loosening a tourniquet releases accumulated toxins and clots into circulation, risking cardiac arrest. Once applied, NEVER loosen or remove — only medical professionals in a hospital should do so.

workarounds.action: For severe limb bleeding: apply tourniquet 2-3 inches above the wound (never on a joint). Tighten until bleeding stops completely. Note the time of application. Do NOT remove. Get to hospital immediately.

If no commercial tourniquet available: use a belt, shirt, or cloth strip (at least 1.5 inches wide). Wrap tightly and use a stick/pen as a windlass to twist and tighten. Narrow cords (shoelaces, wire) cause tissue damage.
